translations.text: And Samuel did what Jehovah said, and came to Bethlehem. And the elders of the city came trembling to meet him, and said, Dost thou come peaceably?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Peaceably: I am come to sacrifice to Jehovah. Hallow yourselves, and come with me to the sacrifice. And he hallowed Jesse and his sons, and called them to the sacrifice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when they were come, that he looked on Eliab, and said, Surely Jehovah's anointed is before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah said to Samuel, Look not on his countenance, or on the height of his stature; because I have rejected him; for it is not as man seeth; for man looketh upon the outward appearance, but Jehovah looketh upon the heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jesse called Abinadab, and made him pass before Samuel. And he said, Neither has Jehovah chosen this one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jesse made Shammah pass by. And he said, Neither has Jehovah chosen this one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesse made seven of his sons pass before Samuel. And Samuel said to Jesse, Jehovah has not chosen these.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samuel said to Jesse, Are these all the young men? And he said, There is yet the youngest remaining, and behold, he is feeding the sheep. And Samuel said to Jesse, Send and fetch him; for we will not sit at table till he come hither.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent and brought him in. And he was ruddy, and besides of a lovely countenance and beautiful appearance. And Jehovah said, Arise, anoint him; for this is he.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samuel took the horn of oil, and anointed him in the midst of his brethren. And the Spirit of Jehovah came upon David from that day forward. And Samuel rose up, and went to Ramah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Spirit of Jehovah departed from Saul, and an evil spirit from Jehovah troubled him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul's servants said to him, Behold now, an evil spirit fromGod troubles thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let our lord now speak; thy servants are before thee: they shall seek out a man, a skilful player on a harp; and it shall come to pass, when the evil spirit fromGod is upon thee, that he shall play with his hand, and thou shalt be well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said to his servants, Provide me now a man that can play well, and bring him to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one of the young men answered and said, Behold, I have seen a son of Jesse the Bethlehemite, who is skilled in playing, and he is a valiant man and a man of war, and skilled in speech, and of good presence, and Jehovah is with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Saul sent messengers to Jesse and said, Send me David thy son, who is with the sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesse took an ass with bread, and a flask of wine, and a kid, and sent [them] by David his son to Saul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David came to Saul, and stood before him; and he loved him greatly; and he became his armour-bearer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul sent to Jesse, saying, Let David, I pray thee, stand before me; for he has found favour in my sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when the [evil] spirit fromGod was upon Saul, that David took the harp, and played with his hand; and Saul was refreshed, and was well, and the evil spirit departed from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines assembled their armies to battle, and were gathered together at Sochoh, which belongs to Judah, and encamped between Sochoh and Azekah, in Ephesdammim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul and the men of Israel were gathered together, and encamped in the valley of terebinths, and set the battle in array against the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines stood on the mountain on the one side, and Israel stood on the mountain on the other side; and the ravine was between them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there went out a champion from the camp of the Philistines, named Goliath, of Gath, whose height was six cubits and a span.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he had a helmet of bronze upon his head, and he was clothed with a corselet of scales; and the weight of the corselet was five thousand shekels of bronze.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he had greaves of bronze upon his legs, and a javelin of bronze between his shoulders.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the shaft of his spear was like a weaver's beam; and his spear's head weighed six hundred shekels of iron; and the shield-bearer went before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he stood and cried to the ranks of Israel, and said to them, Why are ye come out to set your battle in array? am not I the Philistine, and ye servants of Saul? choose for yourselves a man, and let him come down to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If he be able to fight with me, and to smite me, then will we be your servants; but if I overcome and smite him, then shall ye be our servants and serve us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistine said, I have defied the ranks of Israel this day; give me a man, that we may fight together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul and all Israel heard these words of the Philistine, and they were dismayed and greatly afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now David was the son of that Ephrathite of Bethlehem-Judah whose name was Jesse; and he had eight sons; and the man was old in the days of Saul, advanced [in years] among men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the three eldest of the sons of Jesse had gone and followed Saul to the battle; and the names of his three sons that went to the battle were Eliab the firstborn, and the second to him Abinadab, and the third Shammah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David was the youngest; and the three eldest had followed Saul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But David went and returned from Saul to feed his father's sheep at Bethlehem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistine drew near morning and evening, and presented himself forty days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesse said to David his son, Take, I pray, for thy brethren, this ephah of parched [corn] and these ten loaves, and carry them quickly to the camp to thy brethren;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and carry these ten cheeses to the captain of the thousand, and visit thy brethren to see how they are, and take a pledge of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Saul, and they, and all the men of Israel [were] in the valley of terebinths, fighting against the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David rose up early in the morning, and left the sheep with a keeper, and took his charge and went, as Jesse had commanded him. And he came to the wagon-defence; and the host which was going forth to the battle-array shouted for the fight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel and the Philistines put the battle in array, rank against rank.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David left the things he was carrying in the hand of the keeper of the baggage, and ran into the ranks, and came and saluted his brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he talked with them, behold there came up the champion, the Philistine of Gath, Goliath by name, out of the ranks of the Philistines, and spoke according to the same words; and David heard [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the men of Israel, when they saw the man, fled from him and were greatly afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Israel said, Have ye seen this man that comes up? for to defy Israel is he come up: and it shall be, that the man who smites him, him will the king enrich with great riches, and will give him his daughter, and make his father's house free in Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David spoke to the men that stood by him, saying, What shall be done to the man that smites this Philistine, and takes away the reproach from Israel? for who is this uncircumcised Philistine, that he should defy the armies of the livingGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people told him after this manner, saying, So shall it be done to the man that smites him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Eliab, his eldest brother, heard while he spoke to the men; and Eliab's anger was kindled against David, and he said, Why art thou come down? and with whom hast thou left those few sheep in the wilderness? I know thy pride and the naughtiness of thy heart; for thou art come down that thou mightest see the battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said, What have I now done? Was it not laid upon me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he turned from him to another, and spoke after the same manner; and the people answered him again after the former manner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the words were heard which David spoke, and they rehearsed them before Saul; and he sent for him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Saul, Let no man's heart fail because of him: thy servant will go and fight with this Philistine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said to David, Thou art not able to go against this Philistine to fight with him; for thou art but a youth, and he a man of war from his youth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Saul, Thy servant fed his father's sheep, and there came a lion, and also a bear, and took a lamb out of the flock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I went after him, and smote him, and delivered it out of his mouth; and when he arose against me, I seized him by his beard, and smote him, and slew him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy servant smote both the lion and the bear; and this uncircumcised Philistine shall be as one of them, because he has defied the armies of the livingGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said, Jehovah who delivered me out of the paw of the lion and out of the paw of the bear, he will deliver me out of the hand of this Philistine. And Saul said to David, Go, and Jehovah be with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul clothed David with his dress, and put a helmet of bronze upon his head, and clothed him with a corselet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David girded his sword upon his dress, and endeavoured to go; for he had not yet tried [it]. And David said to Saul, I cannot go in these; for I have never tried [them]. And David put them off him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took his staff in his hand, and chose him five smooth stones out of the brook, and put them in the shepherd's bag that he had, into the pocket; and his sling was in his hand. And he drew near to the Philistine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistine came on and approached David; and the man that bore the shield was before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the Philistine looked about and saw David, he disdained him; for he was a youth, and ruddy, and besides of a beautiful countenance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistine said to David, Am I a dog, that thou comest to me with staves? And the Philistine cursed David by hisgods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistine said to David, Come to me, and I will give thy flesh to the fowls of the heavens and to the beasts of the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to the Philistine, Thou comest to me with sword, and with spear, and with javelin; but I come to thee in the name of Jehovah of hosts, theGod of the armies of Israel, whom thou hast defied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This day will Jehovah deliver thee up into my hand; and I will smite thee, and take thy head from thee; and I will give the carcases of the camp of the Philistines this day to the fowl of the heavens and to the wild beasts of the earth. And all the earth shall know that Israel has aGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all this congregation shall know that Jehovah saves not with sword and spear; for the battle is Jehovah's, and he will give you into our hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when the Philistine arose, and came and advanced to meet David, that David hasted, and ran towards the ranks to meet the Philistine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David put his hand into the bag, and took thence a stone, and slang it, and smote the Philistine in his forehead, and the stone sank into his forehead; and he fell on his face to the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So David overcame the Philistine with a sling and a stone, and smote the Philistine and killed him; and there was no sword in the hand of David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David ran, and stood upon the Philistine, and took his sword, and drew it out of its sheath, and killed him completely, and cut off his head with it. And when the Philistines saw that their hero was dead, they fled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of Israel and of Judah arose, and shouted, and pursued the Philistines, until thou comest to the ravine and to the gates of Ekron. And the wounded of the Philistines fell down on the way to Shaaraim, even to Gath, and to Ekron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel returned from chasing after the Philistines, and they pillaged their camps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David took the head of the Philistine and brought it to Jerusalem; but he put his armour in his tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Saul saw David go forth against the Philistine, he said to Abner, the captain of the host, Abner, whose son is this young man? And Abner said, As thy soul liveth, O king, I cannot tell.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said, Inquire thou whose son this youth is.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as David returned from the slaughter of the Philistine, Abner took him, and brought him before Saul with the head of the Philistine in his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said to him, Whose son art thou, young man? And David said, I am the son of thy servant Jesse the Beth-lehemite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when he had ended speaking to Saul, that the soul of Jonathan was knit with the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as his own soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul took him that day, and would not let him return to his father's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan and David made a covenant, because he loved him as his own soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan stripped himself of the robe that was upon him, and gave it to David, and his dress, even to his sword, and to his bow, and to his girdle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David went forth; whithersoever Saul sent him he prospered; and Saul set him over the men of war, and he was accepted in the sight of all the people, and also in the sight of Saul's servants.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as they came, when David returned from the slaughter of the Philistine, that the women came out of all the cities of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet king Saul, with tambours, with joy, and with triangles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the women answered [one another] as they played, and said, Saul hath smitten his thousands, And David his ten thousands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul was very wroth, and that saying was evil in his sight; and he said, They have ascribed to David ten thousands, but to me they have ascribed the thousands; and [what] is there more for him but the kingdom?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul eyed David from that day and forward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the next day that an evil spirit fromGod came upon Saul, and he prophesied in the midst of the house, but David played with his hand, as on other days; and the spear was in Saul's hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul cast the spear, and thought, I will smite David and the wall. But David turned away from him twice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul was afraid of David, because Jehovah was with him, and had departed from Saul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul removed him from him, and made him his captain over a thousand; and he went out and came in before the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David prospered in all his ways; and Jehovah was with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul saw that he prospered well, and he stood in awe of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But all Israel and Judah loved David, for he went out and came in before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said to David, Behold my eldest daughter Merab, her will I give thee to wife; only be thou valiant for me, and fight Jehovah's battles. But Saul thought, My hand shall not be upon him, but the hand of the Philistines shall be upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Saul, Who am I? and what is my life, [or] my father's family in Israel, that I should be son-in-law to the king?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the time when Merab Saul's daughter should have been given to David, that she was given to Adriel the Meholathite as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Michal Saul's daughter loved David; and they told Saul, and the thing was right in his sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said, I will give him her, that she may be a snare to him, and that the hand of the Philistines may be upon him. And Saul said to David, Thou shalt this day be my son-in-law a second time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul commanded his servants, Speak with David secretly, saying, Behold, the king has delight in thee, and all his servants love thee: now therefore be the king's son-in-law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul's servants spoke those words in the ears of David. And David said, Is it a light thing in your eyes to be the king's son-in-law, seeing that I am a poor man, and lightly esteemed?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servants of Saul told him, saying, On this manner did David speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said, Thus shall ye say to David: The king does not desire any dowry, but a hundred foreskins of the Philistines, to be avenged of the king's enemies. But Saul thought to make David fall by the hand of the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his servants told David these words; and the thing was right in David's sight to be the king's son-in-law. And the days were not expired,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when David arose and went, he and his men, and smote of the Philistines two hundred men; and David brought their foreskins, and they delivered them in full to the king, that he might be the king's son-in-law. And Saul gave him Michal his daughter as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul saw and knew that Jehovah was with David; and Michal Saul's daughter loved him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul was yet the more afraid of David; and Saul was David's enemy continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the princes of the Philistines went forth; and it came to pass, whenever they went forth, that David succeeded better than all the servants of Saul; and his name was much esteemed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul spoke to Jonathan his son, and to all his servants, that they should slay David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jonathan Saul's son delighted much in David. And Jonathan told David, saying, Saul my father seeks to kill thee; and now, I pray thee, take heed to thyself in the morning, and abide in a secret place and hide thyself;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will go out and stand beside my father in the field where thou art, and will speak of thee with my father: and see what it is, and tell thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan spoke good of David to Saul his father, and said to him, Let not the king sin against his servant, against David, because he has not sinned against thee; for also what he did was very advantageous to thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he put his life in hand, and smote the Philistine, and Jehovah wrought a great salvation for all Israel: thou didst see [it], and didst rejoice; why then wilt thou sin against innocent blood, in slaying David without cause?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul hearkened to the voice of Jonathan, and Saul swore, [As] Jehovah liveth, he shall not be put to death!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jonathan called David, and Jonathan declared to him all those things. And Jonathan brought David to Saul, and he was in his presence as previously.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was war again; and David went forth and fought with the Philistines, and smote them with a great slaughter; and they fled before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And an evil spirit from Jehovah was upon Saul. And he sat in his house with his spear in his hand; and David played with his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul sought to smite David and the wall with the spear; but he slipped away out of Saul's presence, and he smote the spear into the wall. And David fled, and escaped that night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul sent messengers to David's house, to watch him, and to slay him in the morning; and Michal David's wife told him, saying, If thou save not thy life to-night, to-morrow thou wilt be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Michal let David down through a window; and he went, and fled and escaped.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Michal took the image, and laid it in the bed, and put the net of goats' [hair] at its head, and covered it with the coverlet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul sent messengers to take David, and she said, He is sick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul sent the messengers to see David, saying, Bring him up to me in the bed, that I may put him to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the messengers came in, and behold, the image was in the bed, and the net of goats' [hair] at its head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Saul said to Michal, Why hast thou deceived me so, and sent away mine enemy, that he is escaped? And Michal said to Saul, He said to me, Let me go; why should I slay thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David fled, and escaped, and came to Samuel to Ramah, and told him all that Saul had done to him. And he and Samuel went and dwelt in Naioth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told Saul, saying, Behold, David is at Naioth by Ramah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Saul sent messengers to take David; and they saw a company of prophets prophesying, and Samuel standing as president over them; and the Spirit ofGod came upon the messengers of Saul, and they also prophesied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told Saul, and he sent other messengers, and they also prophesied. And Saul sent messengers again the third time, and they also prophesied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then went he also to Ramah, and came to the great well that is in Sechu; and he asked and said, Where are Samuel and David? And one said, Behold, at Naioth by Ramah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went thither to Naioth by Ramah; and the Spirit ofGod came upon him also, and he went on, and prophesied, until he came to Naioth by Ramah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he himself also stripped off his clothes, and prophesied, himself also, before Samuel, and lay down naked all that day and all that night. Wherefore they say, Is Saul also among the prophets?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David fled from Naioth by Ramah, and came and said before Jonathan, What have I done? what is mine iniquity, and what is my sin before thy father, that he seeks my life?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him,God forbid; thou shalt not die: behold, my father will do nothing either great or small, and not apprise me; and why should my father hide this thing from me? it is not so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David swore again and again, and said, Thy father certainly knows that I have found favour in thy sight: and he has thought, Jonathan shall not know this, lest he be grieved; but truly [as] Jehovah liveth, and [as] thy soul liveth, there is but a step between me and death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan said to David, What thy soul may say, I will even do it for thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Jonathan, Behold, to-morrow is new moon, and I should not fail to sit with the king at meat; but let me go, that I may hide myself in the field until the third evening.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thy father should actually miss me, then say, David earnestly asked leave of me that he might run to Bethlehem his city; for there is a yearly sacrifice there for all the family.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If he say thus, It is well, — thy servant shall have peace; but if he be very wroth, be sure that evil is determined by him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deal kindly then with thy servant; for thou hast brought thy servant into a covenant of Jehovah with thee; but if there be in me iniquity, slay me thyself; for why shouldest thou bring me to thy father?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan said, Far be it from thee; for, if I knew with certainty that evil were determined by my father to come upon thee, would I not tell it thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said David to Jonathan, Who shall tell me? or what if thy father answer thee roughly?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan said to David, Come and let us go out into the field. And they went out both of them into the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan said to David, Jehovah,God of Israel, when I sound my father about this time to-morrow, [or] the next day, and behold, there be good toward David, and I then send not to thee, and apprise thee of it,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah do so and much more to Jonathan. Should it please my father [to do] thee evil, then I will apprise thee of it, and send thee away, that thou mayest go in peace; and Jehovah be with thee, as he has been with my father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt not only while yet I live shew me the kindness of Jehovah, that I die not,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but thou shalt not cut off thy kindness from my house for ever, no, not when Jehovah cuts off the enemies of David, every one from the face of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan made [a covenant] with the house of David, [saying,] Let Jehovah even require [it] at the hand of David's enemies!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan caused David to swear again, by the love he had for him, for he loved him as he loved his own soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan said to him, To-morrow is the new moon; and thou wilt be missed, for thy seat will be empty;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but on the third day thou shalt go down quickly, and come to the place where thou didst hide thyself on the day of the business, and abide by the stone Ezel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will shoot three arrows on the side of it, as though I shot at a mark.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, I will send the lad, [saying,] Go, find the arrows. If I expressly say to the lad, Behold, the arrows are on this side of thee, take them; then come, for there is peace for thee, and it is nothing; [as] Jehovah liveth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if I say thus to the youth: Behold, the arrows are beyond thee, — go thy way; for Jehovah sends thee away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as to the matter which thou and I have spoken of, behold, Jehovah is between me and thee for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David hid himself in the field; and it was the new moon, and the king sat at table to eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king sat on his seat, as at other times, on the seat by the wall; and Jonathan arose, and Abner sat by Saul's side, and David's place was empty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said nothing that day; for he thought, Something has befallen [him], that he is not clean: surely he is not clean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the next day after the new moon, the second [day of the month], as David's place was empty, that Saul said to Jonathan his son, Why has not the son of Jesse come to table, neither yesterday nor to-day?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan answered Saul, David earnestly asked leave of me [to go] to Bethlehem,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, Let me go, I pray thee; for we have a family sacrifice in the city; and my brother himself has commanded me [to be there]; and now, if I have found favour in thy sight, let me go away, I pray thee, and see my brethren. He has therefore not come to the king's table.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul's anger was kindled against Jonathan, and he said to him, Son of the perverse rebellious woman, do not I know that thou hast chosen the son of Jesse to thine own shame and to the shame of thy mother's nakedness?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as long as the son of Jesse lives upon earth, thou shalt not be established, nor thy kingdom. And now send and fetch him to me, for he must die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan answered Saul his father, and said to him, Why should he be put to death? what has he done?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Saul cast the spear at him to smite him; and Jonathan knew that it was determined by his father to put David to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan arose from the table in fierce anger, and ate no meat the second day of the new moon; for he was grieved for David, because his father had done him shame.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the morning that Jonathan went out into the field, to the place agreed on with David, and a little lad with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to his lad, Run, find now the arrows which I shoot. The lad ran, and he shot the arrow beyond him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the lad came to the place of the arrow which Jonathan had shot, Jonathan cried after the lad and said, Is not the arrow away beyond thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan cried after the lad, Make speed, haste, stay not! And Jonathan's lad gathered up the arrows, and came to his master.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the lad knew nothing: only Jonathan and David knew the matter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan gave his weapons to his lad, and said to him, Go, carry them to the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lad went, and David arose from the side of the south, and fell on his face to the ground, and bowed himself three times; and they kissed one another, and wept one with another, until David exceeded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan said to David, Go in peace, forasmuch as we have both of us sworn in the name of Jehovah, saying, Jehovah be between me and thee, and between my seed and thy seed for ever! And he arose and departed; and Jonathan went into the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David came to Nob, to Ahimelech the priest; and Ahimelech trembled at meeting David, and said to him, Why art thou alone, and no man with thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Ahimelech the priest, The king has commanded me a business, and has said to me, Let no man know anything of the business whereon I send thee, and what I have commanded thee; and I have directed the young men to such and such a place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now what is under thy hand? give me five loaves in my hand, or what may be found.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest answered David and said, There is no common bread under my hand, but there is holy bread; if the young men have kept themselves at least from women.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David answered the priest and said to him, Yes indeed, women have been kept from us about these three days, since I came out, and the vessels of the young men are holy, and the [bread] is in a manner common, and the more so, because to-day [new] is hallowed in the vessels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest gave him holy [bread]; for there was no bread there but the shew-loaves that were taken from before Jehovah, to put on hot bread in the day when they were taken away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (Now a certain man of the servants of Saul was there that day, detained before Jehovah; and his name was Doeg, the Edomite, chief of the shepherds that [belonged] to Saul.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Ahimelech, And is there not here under thy hand spear or sword? for I have neither brought my sword nor my weapons with me, for the king's business was urgent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest said, The sword of Goliath the Philistine, whom thou slewest in the valley of terebinths, behold, it is here wrapped in a cloth behind the ephod; if thou wilt take that, take it; for there is no other save that here. And David said, There is none like that: give it me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David arose, and fled that day from before Saul, and went to Achish the king of Gath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servants of Achish said to him, Is not this David the king of the land? did they not sing one to another of him in dances, saying, Saul has smitten his thousands, and David his ten thousands?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David took to heart these words, and was much afraid of Achish the king of Gath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he changed his behaviour before them, and feigned himself mad in their hands, and scratched on the doors of the gate, and let his spittle fall down on his beard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Achish said to his servants, Behold, ye see the man is mad: why did ye bring him to me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: have I lack of madmen, that ye have brought this one to rave in my presence? shall this [man] come into my house?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David departed thence, and escaped to the cave of Adullam. And his brethren and all his father's house heard [it], and they went down thither to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every one in distress, and every one that was in debt, and every one of embittered spirit collected round him; and he became a captain over them; and there were with him about four hundred men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David went thence to Mizpeh in Moab, and said to the king of Moab, Let my father and my mother, I pray thee, come forth amongst you, till I know whatGod will do for me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought them before the king of Moab; and they abode with him all the while that David was in the stronghold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prophet Gad said to David, Abide not in the stronghold; depart, and go into the land of Judah. Then David departed, and came into the forest of Hareth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul heard that David was discovered, and the men that were with him. Now Saul was sitting in Gibeah under the tamarisk upon the height, having his spear in his hand, and all his servants were standing by him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Saul said to his servants that stood by him, Hear now, ye Benjaminites: will the son of Jesse give every one of you also fields and vineyards? Will he make you all captains of thousands and captains of hundreds,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that all of you have conspired against me, and there is none that informs me when my son has made [a covenant] with the son of Jesse; and there is none of you that is sorry for me, or informs me that my son has stirred up my servant as a lier-in-wait against me, as at this day?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then answered Doeg the Edomite, who was set over the servants of Saul, and said, I saw the son of Jesse coming to Nob, to Ahimelech the son of Ahitub.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he inquired of Jehovah for him, and gave him victuals, and gave him the sword of Goliath the Philistine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the king sent to call Ahimelech the priest, the son of Ahitub, and all his father's house, the priests that were in Nob; and they came all of them to the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said, Hear now, son of Ahitub. And he answered, Here I am, my lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said to him, Why have ye conspired against me, thou and the son of Jesse, in that thou hast given him bread, and a sword, and hast inquired ofGod for him, that he should rise against me as a lier-in-wait, as at this day?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahimelech answered the king and said, And who is so faithful among all thy servants as David, who is the king's son-in-law, and has access to thy secret council, and is honourable in thy house?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Was it to-day that I began to inquire ofGod for him? be it far from me: let not the king charge anything to his servant, [nor] to all the house of my father; for thy servant knew nothing of all this, less or more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said, Thou shalt certainly die, Ahimelech, thou, and all thy father's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said to the couriers that stood about him, Turn and put the priests of Jehovah to death; because their hand also is with David, and because they knew when he fled, and did not inform me. But the servants of the king were not willing to put forth their hand to fall on the priests of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said to Doeg, Turn thou, and fall on the priests. And Doeg the Edomite turned, and fell on the priests, and put to death that day eighty-five persons who wore the linen ephod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nob, the city of the priests, he smote with the edge of the sword, both men and women, infants and sucklings, and oxen, and asses, and sheep, with the edge of the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one of the sons of Ahimelech the son of Ahitub, named Abiathar, escaped, and fled after David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abiathar informed David that Saul had slain Jehovah's priests.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Abiathar, I knew it that day, when Doeg the Edomite was there, that he would certainly tell Saul: I am accountable for all the lives of thy father's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Abide with me, fear not; for he that seeks my life seeks thy life; for with me thou art in safe keeping.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they told David, saying, Behold, the Philistines fight against Keilah, and they rob the threshing-floors.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David inquired of Jehovah, saying, Shall I go and smite these Philistines? And Jehovah said to David, Go and smite the Philistines, and save Keilah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But David's men said to him, Behold, we are afraid here in Judah; how much more then if we go to Keilah against the armies of the Philistines?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David inquired of Jehovah yet again. And Jehovah answered him and said, Arise, go down to Keilah; for I will give the Philistines into thy hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David and his men went to Keilah, and fought against the Philistines, and brought away their cattle, and smote them with a great slaughter. So David saved the inhabitants of Keilah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Abiathar the son of Ahimelech fled to David to Keilah, he came down with an ephod in his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told Saul that David had come to Keilah. Then Saul said,God has cast him off into my hand; for he is shut in, by entering into a city that has gates and bars.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul summoned all the people to war, to go down to Keilah, to besiege David and his men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when David knew that Saul devised mischief against him, he said to Abiathar the priest, Bring the ephod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said David, Jehovah,God of Israel, thy servant hath heard for certain that Saul seeketh to come to Keilah, to destroy the city for my sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will the citizens of Keilah deliver me up into his hand? will Saul come down, as thy servant hath heard? Jehovah,God of Israel, I beseech thee, tell thy servant. And Jehovah said, He will come down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said, Will the citizens of Keilah deliver up me and my men into the hand of Saul? And Jehovah said, They will deliver [thee] up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then David and his men, about six hundred, arose and departed out of Keilah, and went whithersoever they could go. And it was told Saul that David had escaped from Keilah, and he forbore to go forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David abode in the wilderness in strongholds, and abode in the mountain in the wilderness of Ziph. And Saul sought him every day, butGod did not give him into his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David saw that Saul had come out to seek his life; and David was in the wilderness of Ziph in a wood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan Saul's son arose, and went to David into the wood, and strengthened his hand inGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Fear not; for the hand of Saul my father will not find thee; and thou shalt be king over Israel, and I shall be next to thee; and that also Saul my father knows.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they two made a covenant before Jehovah; and David abode in the wood, and Jonathan went to his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Ziphites came up to Saul to Gibeah, saying, Does not David hide himself with us in strongholds in the wood, on the hill of Hachilah, which is on the south of the waste?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, O king, come down according to all the desire of thy soul to come down; and it will be for us to deliver him up into the king's hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said, Blessed be ye of Jehovah; for ye have compassion upon me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go, I pray you, make yet more sure, and know and see his place where his track is, who has seen him there; for it is told me that he deals very subtilly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And see, and ascertain all the lurking-places where he hides himself, and come ye again to me with sure information, that I may go with you; and it shall come to pass, if he be in the land, that I will search him out throughout the thousands of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they arose and went to Ziph before Saul; but David and his men were in the wilderness of Maon, in the plain on the south of the waste.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul and his men went to seek [him]. And they told David; and he came down from the rock, and abode in the wilderness of Maon. And Saul heard [that], and he pursued after David in the wilderness of Maon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul went on this side of the mountain, and David and his men on that side of the mountain; and David made haste to get away from Saul; and Saul and his men sought to surround David and his men to take them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But there came a messenger to Saul, saying, Haste thee and come; for the Philistines have made a raid against the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul returned from pursuing after David, and went against the Philistines; therefore they called that place Sela-hammahlekoth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David went up from thence, and abode in the strongholds of Engedi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Saul had returned from following the Philistines, that it was told him, saying, Behold, David is in the wilderness of Engedi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul took three thousand men, chosen out of all Israel, and went to seek David and his men upon the rocks of the wild goats.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came to the sheepfolds by the way, where was a cave; and Saul went in to cover his feet; and David and his men were abiding in the recesses of the cave.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David's men said to him, Behold the day of which Jehovah said to thee, Behold, I will give thine enemy into thy hand, that thou mayest do to him as it shall seem good to thee. And David arose, and cut off the skirt of Saul's robe secretly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass afterwards that David's heart smote him, because he had cut off Saul's skirt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to his men, Jehovah forbid that I should do this thing to my master, Jehovah's anointed, to stretch forth my hand against him, for he is the anointed of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David checked his men with these words, and suffered them not to rise against Saul. And Saul rose up out of the cave, and went on [his] way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: David also arose afterwards, and went out of the cave, and cried after Saul, saying, My lord the king! And when Saul looked behind him, David bowed with his face to the earth, and did obeisance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Saul, Why dost thou listen to words of men, saying, Behold, David seeks thy hurt?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, this day thine eyes have seen how that Jehovah had given thee this day into my hand in the cave; and they bade me kill thee; but [mine eye] spared thee; and I said, I will not put forth my hand against my lord, for he is the anointed of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And see, my father, yes, see the skirt of thy robe in my hand. For in that I cut off the skirt of thy robe, and killed thee not, know and see that there is neither evil nor transgression in my hand, and I have not sinned against thee; yet thou liest in wait for my life to take it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah judge between me and thee, and Jehovah avenge me of thee; but my hand shall not be upon thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As saith the proverb of the ancients, Wickedness proceedeth from the wicked; but my hand shall not be upon thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After whom is the king of Israel come out? after whom dost thou pursue? after a dead dog, after a single flea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah therefore shall be judge, and judge between me and thee, and see, and plead my cause, and do me justice [in delivering me] out of thy hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as soon as David had ended speaking these words to Saul, Saul said, Is this thy voice, my son David? And Saul lifted up his voice and wept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to David, Thou art more righteous than I; for thou hast rewarded me good, whereas I have rewarded thee evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou hast shewed this day how that thou hast dealt well with me, forasmuch as when Jehovah had delivered me up into thy hand, thou didst not kill me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if a man find his enemy, will he let him go well away? wherefore Jehovah reward thee good for that thou hast done to me this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now behold, I know that thou shalt certainly be king, and that the kingdom of Israel shall be established in thy hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Swear now therefore to me by Jehovah, that thou wilt not cut off my seed after me, and that thou wilt not destroy my name out of my father's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David swore to Saul. And Saul went home; and David and his men went up to the stronghold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samuel died; and all Israel were gathered together, and lamented him; and they buried him in his house at Ramah. And David arose and went down to the wilderness of Paran.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a man at Maon, whose business was at Carmel; and the man was very great, and he had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats; and he was shearing his sheep at Carmel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the name of the man was Nabal, and the name of his wife Abigail; and the woman was of good understanding, and of a beautiful countenance; but the man was churlish and evil in his doings; and he was a Calebite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then David sent out ten young men; and David said to the young men, Go up to Carmel, and go to Nabal, and greet him in my name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thus shall ye say: Long life [to thee]! and peace be to thee, and peace be to thy house, and peace be to all that thou hast!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now I have heard that thou hast shearers; now thy shepherds who were with us, we hurt them not, neither was there aught missed by them, all the while they were in Carmel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ask thy young men, and they will tell thee. Therefore let the young men find favour in thine eyes; for we come in a good day: give, I pray thee, what thy hand may find to thy servants, and to thy son David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David's young men came, and spoke to Nabal according to all those words in the name of David, and ceased.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nabal answered David's servants and said, Who is David? and who is the son of Jesse? there are many servants now-a-days that break away every man from his master.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And shall I take my bread, and my water, and my flesh which I have killed for my shearers, and give [it] to men whom I know not whence they are?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David's young men turned their way, and went back, and came and reported to him according to all those words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to his men, Gird ye on every man his sword. And they girded on every man his sword; and David also girded on his sword; and there went up after David about four hundred men; and two hundred abode by the baggage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one of [Nabal's] young men told Abigail, Nabal's wife, saying, Behold, David sent messengers out of the wilderness to bless our master; and he has insulted them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men were very good to us, and we were not hurt, neither missed we anything, as long as we companied with them, when we were in the fields.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They were a wall to us both by night and day, all the while we were with them feeding the sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now know and consider what thou wilt do, for evil is determined against our master, and against all his household; and he is such a son of Belial, that one cannot speak to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abigail made haste, and took two hundred loaves, and two skin-bottles of wine, and five sheep ready dressed, and five measures of parched [corn], and a hundred raisin-cakes, and two hundred fig-cakes, and laid them on asses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to her young men, Go on before me; behold, I come after you. But she did not tell her husband Nabal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as she was riding on the ass, and coming down by the covert of the hill, behold, David and his men came down opposite to her; and she met them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now David had said, Surely, in vain have I kept all that this [man] had in the wilderness, so that nothing was missed of all that was his; and he has requited me evil for good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So and more also doGod to the enemies of David, if I leave of all that is his by the morning light any male.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Abigail saw David, she hasted and lighted off the ass, and fell before David on her face, and bowed herself to the ground,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and fell at his feet, and said, Upon me, my lord, [upon] me let the iniquity be; but let thy handmaid, I pray thee, speak in thine ears, and hear the words of thy handmaid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let not my lord, I pray thee, regard this man of Belial, Nabal; for as his name is, so is he: Nabal is his name, and folly is with him; and I thy handmaid did not see the young men of my lord, whom thou didst send.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, my lord, [as] Jehovah liveth, and [as] thy soul liveth, seeing Jehovah has restrained thee from coming with bloodshed, and from avenging thyself with thine own hand, now let thine enemies, and they that seek evil to my lord, be as Nabal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now this blessing which thy bondmaid has brought to my lord, let it be given to the young men that follow my lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I pray thee, forgive the transgression of thy handmaid: for Jehovah will certainly make my lord a lasting house; because my lord fights the battles of Jehovah, and evil has not been found in thee all thy days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if a man is risen up to pursue thee and to seek thy life, the soul of my lord shall be bound in the bundle of the living with Jehovah thyGod; and the souls of thine enemies, them shall he sling out from the hollow of the sling.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when Jehovah shall do to my lord according to all the good that he has spoken concerning thee, and shall appoint thee ruler over Israel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that this shall be no stumbling-block to thee, nor offence of heart for my lord, either that thou hast shed blood without cause, or that my lord has avenged himself. And when Jehovah shall deal well with my lord, then remember thy handmaid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Abigail, Blessed be Jehovah, theGod of Israel, who sent thee this day to meet me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And blessed be thy discernment, and blessed be thou, who hast kept me this day from coming with bloodshed, and from avenging myself with mine own hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But indeed, as Jehovah theGod of Israel liveth, who has restrained me from hurting thee, except thou hadst hasted and come to meet me, there had not been left to Nabal by the morning light any male.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So David received of her hand what she had brought him, and said to her, Go up in peace to thy house; see, I have hearkened to thy voice, and have accepted thy person.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abigail came to Nabal; and behold, he held a feast in his house, like the feast of a king; and Nabal's heart was merry within him, for he was drunken to excess; so she told him nothing, less or more, until the morning light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the morning, when the wine was gone out of Nabal, that his wife told him these things; and his heart died within him, and he became [as] a stone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in about ten days that Jehovah smote Nabal, and he died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when David heard that Nabal was dead, he said, Blessed be Jehovah, who has pleaded the cause of my reproach from the hand of Nabal, and has kept back his servant from evil; but Jehovah has returned Nabal's evil upon his own head. And David sent and communed with Abigail, to take her as his wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servants of David came to Abigail to Carmel, and spoke to her, saying, David has sent us to thee, to take thee as his wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she arose and bowed herself on her face to the earth, and said, Behold, let thy handmaid be a bondwoman to wash the feet of the servants of my lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abigail hasted, and arose, and rode upon an ass, with five damsels of hers that followed her; and she went after the messengers of David, and became his wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: David had also taken Ahinoam of Jizreel; and they became, even both of them, his wives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Saul had given Michal his daughter, David's wife, to Phalti the son of Laish, who was of Gallim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Ziphites came to Saul to Gibeah, saying, Does not David hide himself in the hill of Hachilah, facing the waste?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul arose, and went down to the wilderness of Ziph, having three thousand chosen men of Israel with him, to seek David in the wilderness of Ziph.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul encamped in the hill of Hachilah, which faces the waste, by the way side. And David abode in the wilderness; and when he saw that Saul had come after him into the wilderness,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: David sent out spies, and learned that Saul was certainly come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David arose and came to the place where Saul had encamped; and David beheld the place where Saul lay, and Abner the son of Ner, the captain of his host; and Saul lay within the wagon-defence, and the people were encamped round about him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David spake and said to Ahimelech the Hittite, and to Abishai the son of Zeruiah, Joab's brother, saying, Who will go down with me to Saul to the camp? And Abishai said, I will go down with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David and Abishai came to the people by night, and behold, Saul lay sleeping within the wagon-defence, and his spear stuck in the ground at his head; and Abner and the people lay round about him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abishai said to David,God has delivered thine enemy into thy hand this day; and now let me smite him, I pray thee, with the spear, even to the ground once, and I will not do it the second time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Abishai, Destroy him not; for who can stretch forth his hand against Jehovah's anointed, and be guiltless?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said, [As] Jehovah liveth, Jehovah will surely smite him; either his day shall come to die, or he shall descend into battle and perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah forbid that I should stretch forth my hand against Jehovah's anointed! But now take, I pray thee, the spear that is at his head, and the cruse of water, and let us go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David took the spear and the cruse of water from Saul's head; and they went away, and no man saw [it], and none knew [it], and none awaked, for they were all asleep; for a deep sleep from Jehovah had fallen upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David went over to the other side, and stood on the top of a hill afar off; a great space [being] between them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David cried to the people, and to Abner the son of Ner, saying, Answerest thou not, Abner? Then Abner answered and said, Who art thou that criest to the king?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Abner, Art not thou a man? and who is like to thee in Israel? and why hast thou not guarded thy lord the king? for one of the people came in to destroy the king thy lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This thing is not good which thou hast done. As Jehovah liveth, ye are worthy to die, because ye have not guarded your master, Jehovah's anointed. And now see where the king's spear is, and the cruse of water that was at his head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul knew David's voice, and said, Is this thy voice, my son David? And David said, It is my voice, my lord, O king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Why does my lord thus pursue after his servant? for what have I done? or what evil is in my hand?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, I pray thee, let my lord the king hear the words of his servant. If Jehovah have moved thee against me, let him accept an oblation; but if the sons of men, cursed be they before Jehovah; for they have driven me out this day from adhering to the inheritance of Jehovah, saying, Go, serve othergods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, let not my blood fall to the earth far from the face of Jehovah; for the king of Israel is come out to seek a single flea, as when they hunt a partridge on the mountains.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said, I have sinned: return, my son David; for I will no more do thee harm, because my life was precious in thine eyes this day: behold, I have acted foolishly, and have erred exceedingly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David answered and said, Behold the king's spear, and let one of the young men come over and fetch it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah will render to every man his righteousness and his faithfulness; for Jehovah gave thee into [my] hand this day, and I would not stretch forth my hand against Jehovah's anointed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, as thy life was highly esteemed this day in mine eyes, so let my life be highly esteemed in the eyes of Jehovah, that he may deliver me out of all distress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul said to David, Blessed be thou, my son David: thou shalt certainly do [great things], and shalt certainly prevail. And David went on his way, and Saul returned to his place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said in his heart, I shall now perish one day by the hand of Saul: there is nothing better for me than that I should speedily escape into the land of the Philistines; and Saul will despair of me to seek me any more within all the limits of Israel, and I shall escape out of his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David arose and passed over, he and the six hundred men that were with him, to Achish, the son of Maoch, king of Gath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David abode with Achish at Gath, he and his men, every man with his household; David with his two wives, Ahinoam the Jizreelitess, and Abigail the Carmelitess, Nabal's wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told Saul that David had fled to Gath; and he sought no more for him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Achish, If now I have found favour in thine eyes, let them give me a place in some country-town, that I may abide there; for why should thy servant abide in the royal city with thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Achish gave him Ziklag that day; therefore Ziklag belongs to the kings of Judah to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the time that David abode in the country of the Philistines was a year and four months.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David and his men went up and made a raid upon the Geshurites, and the Gerzites, and the Amalekites: for those were of old the inhabitants of the land, as thou goest to Shur, and as far as the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David smote the land, and left neither man nor woman alive, and took away the sheep, and the oxen, and the asses, and the camels, and the apparel, and returned, and came to Achish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So Achish said, Have ye not made a raid to-day? And David said, Against the south of Judah, and against the south of the Jerahmeelites, and against the south of the Kenites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David left neither man nor woman alive, to bring [them] to Gath, for he said, Lest they should tell of us, saying, So did David. And such was his custom as long as he abode in the country of the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Achish trusted David, saying, He has made himself utterly odious among his people Israel; and he shall be my servant for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in those days that the Philistines gathered together their armies for warfare to fight against Israel. And Achish said to David, Know thou assuredly that thou shalt go out with me to the camp, thou and thy men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Achish, Thereby thou shalt know what thy servant can do. And Achish said to David, Therefore will I make thee keeper of my person for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (Now Samuel was dead, and all Israel had lamented him, and they had buried him in Ramah, even in his own city. And Saul had put away the necromancers and the soothsayers out of the land.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines gathered themselves together, and came and encamped in Shunem; and Saul gathered all Israel together, and they encamped in Gilboa.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Saul saw the camp of the Philistines, he was afraid, and his heart greatly trembled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul inquired of Jehovah; but Jehovah did not answer him, either by dreams, or by Urim, or by prophets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said Saul to his servants, Seek me a woman that has a spirit of Python, that I may go to her and inquire of her. And his servants said to him, Behold, there is a woman who has a spirit of Python at En-dor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul disguised himself, and put on other garments, and he went, and two men with him, and they came to the woman by night; and he said, I pray thee, divine to me by the spirit of Python, and bring me [him] up whom I shall name to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the woman said to him, Behold, thou knowest what Saul has done, how he has cut off the necromancers and the soothsayers out of the land; and why layest thou a snare for my life, to cause me to die?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul swore unto her by Jehovah, saying, [As] Jehovah liveth, there shall no punishment happen to thee for this thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said the woman, Whom shall I bring up to thee? And he said, Bring me up Samuel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the woman saw Samuel, she cried out with a loud voice; and the woman spoke to Saul, saying, Why hast thou deceived me? Even thou art Saul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said to her, Be not afraid; but what didst thou see? And the woman said to Saul, I saw agod ascending out of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, What is his form? And she said, An old man comes up; and he is covered with a mantle. And Saul knew that it was Samuel, and he stooped with his face to the ground, and bowed himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samuel said to Saul, Why hast thou disquieted me, to bring me up? And Saul said, I am sore distressed; for the Philistines make war against me, andGod is departed from me, and answers me no more, neither by prophets nor by dreams; therefore I have called thee, that thou mayest make known to me what I shall do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Samuel said, Why then dost thou inquire of me, seeing Jehovah is departed from thee, and is become thine enemy?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah has done for himself as he spoke by me; and Jehovah has rent the kingdom out of thy hand, and given it to thy neighbour, to David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because thou didst not hearken to the voice of Jehovah, and didst not execute his fierce anger upon Amalek, therefore has Jehovah done this thing to thee this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah will also give Israel with thee into the hand of the Philistines; and to-morrow shalt thou and thy sons be with me; the army of Israel also will Jehovah give into the hand of the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul fell straightway his full length on the earth, and was sore afraid because of the words of Samuel; and there was no strength in him, for he had eaten no bread all the day nor all the night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the woman came to Saul, and saw that he was sore troubled, and said to him, Behold, thy bondmaid has hearkened to thy voice, and I have put my life in my hand, and have hearkened to thy words which thou spokest to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, I pray thee, hearken thou also to the voice of thy bondmaid, and let me set a morsel of bread before thee; and eat, that thou mayest have strength when thou goest on thy way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he refused and said, I will not eat. Then his servants, and the woman also, compelled him, and he hearkened to their voice; and he arose from the earth and sat on the bed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the woman had a fat calf in the house; and she hasted and killed it, and took flour, and kneaded it, and baked unleavened bread thereof;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and she brought it near before Saul, and before his servants, and they ate. And they rose up and went away that night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines gathered together all their armies to Aphek; and Israel encamped by the spring that is in Jizreel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the lords of the Philistines passed on by hundreds and by thousands; and David and his men passed on in the rearward with Achish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the princes of the Philistines said, What are these Hebrews? And Achish said to the princes of the Philistines, Is not this David, the servant of Saul the king of Israel, who has been with me these days, or these years, and I have found nothing in him since the day of his falling away [to me] to this day?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the princes of the Philistines were wroth with him; and the princes of the Philistines said to him, Make the man return, that he may go again to his place where thou hast appointed him, that he go not down with us to the battle, that in the battle he be not an adversary to us; for wherewith should this [fellow] reconcile himself to his master? should it not be with the heads of these men?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not this David, of whom they sang one to another in dances, saying, Saul smote his thousands, and David his ten thousands?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Achish called David, and said to him, [As] Jehovah liveth, thou art upright, and thy going out and thy coming in with me in the camp is acceptable to me; for I have not found evil in thee since the day of thy coming to me to this day; but thou art not acceptable to the lords.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now return, and go in peace, that thou displease not the lords of the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Achish, But what have I done? and what hast thou found in thy servant so long as I have been with thee to this day, that I should not go and fight against the enemies of my lord the king?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Achish answered and said to David, I know that thou art acceptable to me, as an angel ofGod; nevertheless the princes of the Philistines have said, He shall not go up with us to the battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now rise up early in the morning with thy master's servants that are come with thee; and rise ye early in the morning, and when ye have daylight, depart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David rose up early, he and his men, to depart in the morning, to return into the land of the Philistines. And the Philistines went up to Jizreel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when David and his men came to Ziklag on the third day, that the Amalekites had made a raid upon the south, and upon Ziklag, and smitten Ziklag and burned it with fire;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and had taken the women captives that were in it; both great and small: they had put none to death, but had carried them off, and went on their way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David and his men came to the city, and behold, it was burnt with fire; and their wives, and their sons, and their daughters were taken captives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then David and the people that were with him lifted up their voice and wept, until they had no more power to weep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
