translations.text: And David's two wives were taken captives, Ahinoam the Jizreelitess, and Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David was greatly distressed; for the people spoke of stoning him; for the soul of all the people was embittered, every man because of his sons and because of his daughters; but David strengthened himself in Jehovah hisGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Abiathar the priest, Ahimelech's son, Bring near to me, I pray thee, the ephod. And Abiathar brought the ephod near to David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David inquired of Jehovah, saying, Shall I pursue after this troop? shall I overtake them? And he said to him, Pursue; for thou shalt assuredly overtake [them] and shalt certainly recover.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So David went, he and the six hundred men that were with him, and they came to the torrent Besor; and those that were left stayed behind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David pursued, he and four hundred men; for two hundred stayed behind, who were too exhausted to go over the torrent Besor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they found an Egyptian in the field, and brought him to David, and gave him bread, and he ate; and they gave him water to drink,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and gave him a piece of fig-cake and two raisin-cakes, and he ate, and his spirit came again to him; for he had eaten no bread, nor drunk any water, for three days and three nights.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to him, To whom belongest thou? and whence art thou? And he said, I am a young man of Egypt, servant of an Amalekite; and my master left me, because three days ago I fell sick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We made a raid against the south of the Cherethites, and against what [belongs] to Judah, and against the south of Caleb; and we burned Ziklag with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to him, Canst thou bring me down to this troop? And he said, Swear to me byGod, that thou wilt neither put me to death nor deliver me up into the hand of my master, and I will bring thee down to this troop.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought him down, and behold, they were spread over the whole land, eating and drinking, and dancing, because of all the great spoil that they had taken out of the land of the Philistines, and out of the land of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David smote them from the twilight even to the evening of the next day; and there escaped not a man of them, save four hundred young men, who rode upon camels, and fled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David recovered all that the Amalekites had taken: and David recovered his two wives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was nothing missed by them, neither small nor great, neither sons nor daughters, neither spoil nor anything that they had taken: David brought all back.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David took all the flocks and the herds, [which] they drove before the other cattle, and said, This is David's spoil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David came to the two hundred men who had been too exhausted to follow David, and whom they had left behind at the torrent Besor; and they went forth to meet David, and to meet the people that were with him; and David drew near to the people and saluted them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the wicked men, and [men] of Belial, of those that had gone with David, answered and said, Because they went not with us, we will not give them [aught] of the spoil that we have recovered, save to every man his wife and his children, that they may lead [them] away and depart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said David, Ye shall not do so, my brethren, with that which Jehovah has given us, who has preserved us, and given the troop that came against us into our hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And who will hearken to you in this matter? For as his share is that goes down to the battle, so shall his share be that abides by the baggage: they shall share alike.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was [so] from that day forward; and he made it a statute and an ordinance for Israel to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David came to Ziklag, and he sent of the spoil to the elders of Judah, to his friends, saying, Behold a present for you of the spoil of the enemies of Jehovah:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to those in Bethel, and to those in south Ramoth, and to those in Jattir,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to those in Aroer, and to those in Siphmoth, and to those in Eshtemoa,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to those in Rachal, and to those in the cities of the Jerahmeelites, and to those in the cities of the Kenites,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to those in Hormah, and to those in Chor-ashan, and to those in Athach,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to those in Hebron, and to all the places where David himself and his men went about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines fought against Israel; and the men of Israel fled from before the Philistines, and fell down slain on mount Gilboa.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines followed hard upon Saul and upon his sons; and the Philistines smote Jonathan, and Abinadab, and Malchishua, Saul's sons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the battle went sore against Saul, and the archers came up with him; and he was much terrified by the archers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said Saul to his armour-bearer, Draw thy sword, and thrust me through with it; lest these uncircumcised come and thrust me through, and abuse me. But his armour-bearer would not; for he was much afraid. So Saul took the sword and fell on it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when his armour-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he fell likewise on his sword, and died with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So Saul died, and his three sons, and his armour-bearer, and all his men, that same day together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the men of Israel that were on this side of the valley, and [they] that were on this side of the Jordan, saw that the men of Israel fled, and that Saul and his sons were dead, they forsook the cities and fled; and the Philistines came and dwelt in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the next day, that the Philistines came to strip the slain, and they found Saul and his three sons fallen on mount Gilboa.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they cut off his head, and stripped off his armour, and sent [them] into the land of the Philistines round about, to announce the glad tidings in the houses of their idols, and to the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they put his armour in the house of Ashtaroth; and they fastened his body to the wall of Beth-shan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the inhabitants of Jabesh-Gilead heard of what the Philistines had done to Saul,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: all the valiant men arose and went all night, and took the body of Saul and the bodies of his sons from the wall of Beth-shan, and came to Jabesh, and burned them there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took their bones, and buried them under the tamarisk at Jabesh, and fasted seven days.
